content.text: Every page in the Atlas is a JSON file. The root object defines the metadata, the layout behavior, and the style inheritance for all child elements.
These keys determine how the page appears in the search engine and sidebars:

items: title: String. The main heading and browser tab title.
category: String. Grouping label for the sidebar registry.
description: String. Used for SEO and future card previews.

content.text: One of the most powerful features of the v2 engine is the ability to set a default 'look' for a page.
If you set style: 'text_style' at the root, every nested block will lose its borders and backgrounds to look like an article, unless that block specifically overrides it.
Properties that control the underlying HTML engine:

items: template: Usually base.html. Determines the layout file used.
body_class: String. Injects custom CSS classes into the body (e.g., for page-specific CSS selectors).
extra_assets: Array. List of CSS or JS files in assets/css/ or assets/js/ to load only for this page.
